full_description: Arrange the large, medium, and small blocks from left to right in descending size.
schema: {A} denotes the large block, {B} denotes the medium block, {C} denotes the small block, {a} denotes the arm on the large-block side, {b} denotes the arm that moves the medium block, {c} denotes the arm that moves the small block
preference: num of words should not exceed 30
seen: Arrange {A}, {B}, and {C} from left to right by size.
Keep {A} as the left reference, place {B} to its right, then place {C} to the right of {B}.
Line up {A}, {B}, and {C} from largest to smallest.
Place the blocks in order as {A}, then {B}, then {C}.
Use {b} to position {B} to the right of {A}, then use {c} to position {C} to the right of {B}.
Make a left-to-right row of {A}, {B}, and {C}.
Arrange the blocks in descending size from left to right.
Put {B} next to {A}, and put {C} next to {B}.
Keep {A} where it is and complete the row with {B} and {C}.
Form a straight row with {A} on the left, {B} in the middle, and {C} on the right.
Place the blocks so {A} is left of {B}, and {B} is left of {C}.
Arrange the lineup as large, medium, then small.
Sort the three blocks by size in a single row.
unseen: Arrange {A}, {B}, and {C} in descending size from left to right.
Place {B} to the right of {A}, then place {C} to the right of {B}.
Keep {A} as the left anchor and complete the lineup with {B} and {C}.
Set the three blocks in a row with {A} left, {B} center, and {C} right.
Make {A}, {B}, and {C} form a left-to-right size sequence.
Put the blocks in order so {A} comes before {B}, and {B} comes before {C}.
Build the lineup from largest to smallest, placing each block to the right of the previous one.

seen: find {A} and place {B} to its right
unseen: find {A} and place {B} to its right

seen: find {B} and place {C} to its right
unseen: find {B} and place {C} to its right
